proposed_hypotheses.text: Among the 77 binary features, at least one shows a statistically significant association with objective_response at the cohort level (chi-square p<0.05).
feature_013 (binary, prevalence 64.7%) is associated with a LOWER probability of objective_response (ORR in feature_013=1 patients < ORR in feature_013=0 patients).
feature_067 (binary, prevalence 25.2%) is associated with a LOWER probability of objective_response.
feature_006 (binary, prevalence 28.5%) is associated with a HIGHER probability of objective_response.
feature_007 (binary, prevalence 44.7%) is associated with a HIGHER probability of objective_response.
feature_039 (binary, prevalence 50.0%) is associated with a HIGHER probability of objective_response.

analyses.code: import pandas as pd; from scipy import stats; df=pd.read_parquet('dataset.parquet'); a=df.loc[df['feature_013']==1,'objective_response']; b=df.loc[df['feature_013']==0,'objective_response']; tab=[[a.sum(),len(a)-a.sum()],[b.sum(),len(b)-b.sum()]]; stats.chi2_contingency(tab)
analyses.result_summary: ORR in feature_013=1 (n=32,365) is 0.1540 vs ORR=0.1964 in feature_013=0 (n=17,635). Risk difference -0.0425, OR=0.74, chi-square p=1.11e-33. Strongly significant.
ORR(feature_067=1)=0.1436 vs ORR(feature_067=0)=0.1775. Risk difference -0.0339, OR=0.78, chi-square p=1.99e-18.
ORR(feature_006=1)=0.1819 vs ORR(feature_006=0)=0.1638. Risk difference +0.0182, OR=1.14, chi-square p=1.04e-06.
ORR(feature_007=1)=0.1754 vs ORR(feature_007=0)=0.1637. Risk difference +0.0116, OR=1.09, chi-square p=5.90e-04.
ORR(feature_039=1)=0.1739 vs ORR(feature_039=0)=0.1640. Risk difference +0.0099, OR=1.07, chi-square p=3.41e-03.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_051 (ordinal 0/1/2, mean 0.80, plausibly an ECOG-style performance status) shows a monotonically DECREASING ORR with higher levels (level 0 ORR > level 1 ORR > level 2 ORR).
feature_011 (continuous, range 0-28, plausibly a tumor-burden / sum-of-diameters proxy) is associated with a LOWER probability of objective_response (negative logistic-regression coefficient).
feature_063 (continuous, right-skewed, range 0.03-296.88, plausibly a serum biomarker such as LDH) is associated with a LOWER probability of objective_response.
feature_099 (continuous, range 1.7-5.5) is associated with a HIGHER probability of objective_response.
feature_092 (continuous, range 0-0.798, plausibly a fractional biomarker) is associated with a HIGHER probability of objective_response.

analyses.result_summary: ORR by level: level 0 = 0.2108 (n=17,557); level 1 = 0.1570 (n=24,962); level 2 = 0.1105 (n=7,481). Logistic coefficient on the continuous score = -0.375 (p=1.73e-93). Strong monotonic decrease — consistent with an ECOG-like performance status.
Per-unit logistic coef = -0.0385 (p=1.79e-32); ORR by quintile drops from 0.189 (lowest) to 0.137 (highest). Strongly negative.
Per-unit logistic coef = -0.00489 (p=6.88e-04). Higher feature_063 modestly decreases response probability.
Per-unit logistic coef = +0.0907 (p=1.40e-04). Higher feature_099 modestly increases response probability.
Per-unit logistic coef = +0.166 (p=3.19e-03). Higher feature_092 modestly increases response probability.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_057 (smoking status: current/former/never) is NOT associated with objective_response (overall chi-square p>0.05); ORR is approximately equal across smoking-status categories.
feature_043 (histology: adenocarcinoma vs squamous) is associated with objective_response — squamous patients have a HIGHER ORR than adenocarcinoma patients.
feature_123 (race) is associated with objective_response — white patients have a LOWER ORR than non-white patients on average (overall chi-square p<0.05).
feature_005 (insurance) is associated with objective_response — Medicaid patients have a LOWER ORR than privately-insured patients.
feature_078 (age in years, range 30-90, mean 65) shows NO association with objective_response (no monotonic trend across age quartiles).

analyses.result_summary: ORR: current=0.1723 (n=14,974); former=0.1685 (n=27,567); never=0.1638 (n=7,459). Chi-square p=0.269. Not significant.
ORR squamous=0.1737 (n=13,901) vs adenocarcinoma=0.1671 (n=36,099). Risk difference +0.0066, chi-square p=0.078. Direction matches but not significant at 0.05.

ORR by race: white=0.1655 (n=32,607); asian=0.1772; black=0.1776; hispanic=0.1715; other=0.1854. Overall chi-square p=0.045 (significant). Black-vs-white pairwise risk difference +0.0121, p=0.023; other comparisons NS.
ORR: medicaid=0.1613 (n=6,999); private=0.1720 (n=19,823); medicare=0.1685; uninsured=0.1700. Medicaid-vs-private risk difference -0.0107 (z-test p=0.037). Overall insurance chi-square p=0.232.

proposed_hypotheses.text: After multivariable adjustment for performance status (feature_051), tumor burden (feature_011), and other major prognostic features, feature_013 retains a NEGATIVE independent association with objective_response (adjusted OR < 1, p<0.05).
After multivariable adjustment, feature_067 retains a NEGATIVE independent association with objective_response.
After multivariable adjustment, feature_006 retains a POSITIVE independent association with objective_response.
After multivariable adjustment, feature_051 retains a NEGATIVE independent association with objective_response (adjusted per-SD OR < 1, p<0.05).
After multivariable adjustment, feature_011 retains a NEGATIVE independent association with objective_response.
Race effects (Black vs White, feature_123) PERSIST after multivariable adjustment — Black patients retain a higher ORR than White patients (adjusted OR > 1, p<0.05).
Medicaid insurance is associated with LOWER ORR than private insurance even after multivariable adjustment for prognostic features.

analyses.code: sm.Logit(y, X).fit() with X = intercept + standardized [078,011,063,099,092,051] + binaries [013,067,006,007,039,076,112] + smoking dummies + squamous + race dummies (vs white) + insurance dummies (vs private)
analyses.result_summary: Multivariable logit (n=50,000): feature_011 z OR=0.86 (p<1e-30); feature_051 z OR=0.77 (p<1e-30); feature_013 OR=0.74 (p<1e-30); feature_067 OR=0.77 (p<1e-30); feature_006 OR=1.14 (p<1e-6); feature_007 OR=1.08 (p=9e-4); feature_039 OR=1.08 (p=1.5e-3); feature_063 z OR=0.96 (p=5e-4); feature_099 z OR=1.05 (p=1e-4); feature_092 z OR=1.04 (p=2e-3); race_black OR=1.085 (p=0.029); ins_medicaid OR=0.915 (p=0.019); age, smoking, histology NS. LLR p=5.18e-180, pseudo-R^2=0.020.

analyses.code: Coefficient on feature_013 in multivariable logit
analyses.result_summary: Adjusted OR=0.739 (95% CI 0.704-0.776), p<1e-30. Confirmed negative independent effect.

analyses.code: Coefficient on feature_067 in multivariable logit
analyses.result_summary: Adjusted OR=0.772 (95% CI 0.729-0.817), p<1e-30. Confirmed negative independent effect.

analyses.code: Coefficient on feature_006 in multivariable logit
analyses.result_summary: Adjusted OR=1.139 (95% CI 1.080-1.202), p=1.7e-6. Confirmed positive independent effect.

analyses.code: Coefficients on standardized feature_011 and feature_051 in multivariable logit
analyses.result_summary: feature_051 (z): adjusted OR=0.772 per SD (p<1e-30). feature_011 (z): adjusted OR=0.858 per SD (p<1e-30). Both retain large independent negative effects.

analyses.code: Coefficient on race_black indicator in multivariable logit
analyses.result_summary: Adjusted OR=1.085 (95% CI 1.008-1.167), p=0.029. Black patients retain a small but statistically significant ORR advantage relative to white patients after adjustment.

analyses.code: Coefficient on ins_medicaid indicator in multivariable logit
analyses.result_summary: Adjusted OR=0.915 (95% CI 0.849-0.985), p=0.019. Medicaid retains a small but significant lower-ORR association vs private insurance after adjustment.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Among the eight key predictors (013, 067, 006, 007, 039, 076, 112, 051), there is at least one PAIRWISE INTERACTION on the log-odds of objective_response significant at the Bonferroni-corrected threshold (p < 0.05/28 ≈ 0.0018).
feature_006 and feature_039 show a POSITIVE log-odds interaction on objective_response (their joint effect exceeds the sum of their individual effects).
feature_013 and feature_067 act ADDITIVELY on the log-odds of objective_response (no significant interaction term, p>0.05).

analyses.code: All 28 pairwise interactions among 8 key predictors via sm.Logit
analyses.result_summary: feature_006 x feature_039: OR_interaction=1.20, p=4.15e-4 (PASSES Bonferroni 0.0018). feature_007 x feature_039: OR_int=1.16, p=2.4e-3. feature_006 x feature_007: OR_int=1.16, p=4.3e-3. All other 25 pairs p>0.10.

analyses.code: Pairwise interaction logit feature_013 x feature_067
analyses.result_summary: OR_interaction=1.04, p=0.51. Joint stratified ORRs: both 0 = 20.7%; 013 only = 16.1%; 067 only = 16.5%; both 1 = 13.2%. Effects approximately additive on log-odds.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The three positive predictors feature_006, feature_007, feature_039 act SYNERGISTICALLY: ORR shows a dose-response relationship with the count of these three features set to 1 (sum 0/1/2/3 → monotonically increasing ORR).

When feature_006, feature_007, and feature_039 are simultaneously modeled with all main effects, all three pairwise interactions, and the THREE-WAY product, the three-way interaction term f006*f007*f039 is POSITIVE and statistically significant (p<0.05).
The two negative predictors feature_013 and feature_067 also show a dose-response: ORR decreases monotonically with the count (0/1/2) of these two features set to 1.

analyses.result_summary: ORR by count: 0/3 features = 0.1612 (n=9,882); 1/3 = 0.1642 (n=21,815); 2/3 = 0.1679 (n=15,087); 3/3 = 0.2298 (n=3,216). Trend logistic coef per +1 = +0.091, p=1.24e-10. Monotonic with a sharp jump at 3/3 — strongly inconsistent with a purely additive log-odds model.

Three-way term OR=1.479, p=2.0e-4 (highly significant). All three pairwise terms become NS (p=0.43, 0.96, 0.68) and main effects also NS (p=0.24, 0.54, 0.91) once the 3-way term is included — the apparent pairwise interactions in iter 5 were driven entirely by the 3-way synergy.

ORR by count: 0/2 = 0.2068 (n=13,223); 1/2 = 0.1620 (n=28,605); 2/2 = 0.1319 (n=8,172). Trend logistic coef per +1 = -0.277, p=1.34e-49. Monotonically decreasing — additive on log-odds (consistent with the null pairwise interaction in iter 5).

proposed_hypotheses.text: The three-way feature_006*feature_007*feature_039 synergy on objective_response PERSISTS after adjustment for all other major prognostic features (feature_011, feature_051, feature_063, feature_099, feature_092, feature_013, feature_067) — adjusted OR for the three-way term remains > 1 with p<0.05.
The absolute ORR boost from being '3/3 trio-positive' is similar in magnitude across performance-status strata (feature_051 = 0, 1, 2), i.e. the trio acts on an ABSOLUTE-RISK scale roughly uniformly.

analyses.code: Multivariable logit with all main effects + f006*f007*f039
analyses.result_summary: Adjusted OR for f006*f007*f039 = 1.449 (SE on coef 0.056), p=2.7e-11. Persists after full adjustment; main effects of 006/007/039 individually are NS once the 3-way is in the model.

analyses.code: Stratify by feature_051 (0/1/2), tabulate ORR by trio count
analyses.result_summary: PS=0: ORR(0 trio)=0.197 vs ORR(3 trio)=0.270, abs diff=+0.073. PS=1: 0.151 vs 0.213, diff=+0.062. PS=2: 0.108 vs 0.187, diff=+0.079. Absolute boost is 6-8 ppts across PS strata; the trio's effect is approximately uniform on the absolute-risk scale (steeper on relative scale at low ORR).

proposed_hypotheses.text: The negative association of feature_013 with ORR DOES NOT differ meaningfully across racial subgroups (no significant feature_013 x race interaction).
The negative effect of feature_011 (tumor burden proxy) on ORR DOES NOT differ between adenocarcinoma and squamous histology (no significant interaction).
The negative effect of feature_051 (PS) on ORR DOES NOT differ between adenocarcinoma and squamous histology.
feature_011 and feature_051 act ADDITIVELY on log-odds (no interaction).
There is NO interaction between never-smoker status (feature_057='never') and squamous histology (feature_043) on ORR.

analyses.code: sm.Logit(y, [1, feature_013, race_white, feature_013*race_white]) and stratified ORR table
analyses.result_summary: feature_013 risk-difference is consistently negative across races: white -0.044; black -0.047; hispanic -0.036; asian -0.023; other -0.058. Interaction term feature_013 x race_white: OR_int=0.951, p=0.32. No effect modification.
Interaction OR=0.991, p=0.73. No histology effect modification.
Interaction OR=0.967, p=0.22. No histology effect modification.
Interaction OR=0.991, p=0.50. Tumor-burden and PS act additively on log-odds.
Interaction OR=0.96, p=0.77. ORR among never-smoker squamous patients (n=384) is 0.164, indistinguishable from never-smoker adenocarcinoma (0.164).

proposed_hypotheses.text: The disparity for Medicaid (lower adjusted ORR) and the small benefit for Black race (higher adjusted ORR) act INDEPENDENTLY — there is NO significant Black x Medicaid interaction on objective_response.
The negative effect of feature_013 on ORR DOES NOT differ between current smokers and others (feature_057='current').
The negative effect of feature_013 on ORR DOES NOT differ between never smokers and others.
Among ordinal features 026, 033, 064, 018, 045, 042 (range 0-4 or 0-10), NONE shows a monotonic association with ORR after univariate testing (all p>0.05).

analyses.code: Adjusted logit with black, medicaid, black*medicaid, plus full prognostic adjustment
analyses.result_summary: Joint stratified ORR: not-Black/not-Medicaid 0.1690 (n=37,797); not-Black/Medicaid 0.1601 (n=6,195); Black/not-Medicaid 0.1787 (n=5,204); Black/Medicaid 0.1704 (n=804). Adjusted interaction OR=1.013, p=0.91. No compounded effect.
Interaction OR=0.976, p=0.64. No effect modification by current-smoker status.
Interaction OR=1.064, p=0.37. No effect modification by never-smoker status.

analyses.code: Univariate logits for feature_018, feature_026, feature_033, feature_042, feature_045, feature_064
analyses.result_summary: All six per-unit logistic p-values > 0.05 (range 0.24-0.49, except feature_033 p=0.04 with tiny coef -0.022). ORR varies by <2 ppts across levels of each. None contributes meaningfully to predicting response.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Among rare binary features (prevalence < 2%, n+ between 50 and 1000), NONE shows a chi-square p<0.05 association with objective_response.

A multivariable logistic risk score combining the major signals identified across iterations (feature_011, feature_051, feature_063, feature_099, feature_092, feature_013, feature_067, feature_006, feature_007, feature_039, and the 006*007*039 trio interaction) yields a discrimination AUC > 0.59 and risk deciles whose observed ORR varies AT LEAST 2-fold between top and bottom decile.

analyses.code: Chi-square on each binary feature with prevalence in (0.005, 0.02)
analyses.result_summary: Tested feature_052, feature_008, feature_050, feature_060, feature_116, feature_072, feature_108, feature_100, feature_058, feature_113, feature_080, feature_098, feature_009. Smallest p = 0.116 (feature_100). None significant. Rare features carry no detectable response signal.

analyses.code: sm.Logit on final design; rank-based AUC; deciles of predicted probability
analyses.result_summary: Final-model AIC=44,505, pseudo-R^2=0.0207, rank AUC=0.6045. ORR by predicted-risk decile rises monotonically: D1=0.093; D2=0.110; D3=0.128; D4=0.144; D5=0.158; D6=0.172; D7=0.184; D8=0.214; D9=0.225; D10=0.262. Top vs bottom decile risk difference +0.169 (z=22.65, p<<1e-30) — 2.8x ratio.
